name: Lusitanian
notes: Lusitanian (1st c. BCE). Indo-European language of western Iberia, known from ~5 inscriptions in Latin alphabet (Cabeço das Fráguas, Lamas de Moledo, Arroyo de la Luz). PRESERVES PIE *p (porcom 'pig') — rules out Celtic classification. Has /f/ (possibly < *bh, suggesting Italic affinity). Exact IE branch debated: possibly Italic, para-Celtic, or independent. Key evidence for IE dialectology.
